Tests the creation and properties of an `Animal` object in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code defines a constructor function [`Animal`](<#02-002Animal>) that initializes an object with a `name` property and a `canWalk` property set to `true`. The [`Animal`](<#02-002Animal>) function is used to create an instance named `animal` with the name "animal". The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the `name` property of the `animal` instance is correctly set to "animal". This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on object creation and property initialization for a simple [`Animal`](<#02-002Animal>) object.
# Global Variables

---
### animal
- **Type**: ``Animal``
- **Description**: An instance of the `Animal` class with the name property set to 'animal'. The `Animal` class has a `name` property and a `canWalk` property, which is set to true by default.
- **Use**: Used to create an `Animal` object with the name 'animal' and verify its `name` property.


# Functions

---
### Animal<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.02/11.02.02-002.Animal}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.02/11.02.02-002.js#L15>)

Represents an animal with a name and the ability to walk.
- **Inputs**:
    - `name`: The name of the animal, which is a string.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the input `name` to the `name` property of the object.
    - Sets the `canWalk` property of the object to `true`.
- **Output**: An object with `name` and `canWalk` properties.
